commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer review and landing with refreshed checks.; Cleared: The diff only adds an internal TypeScript logger wrapper and changes one import/call site; it does not touch secrets, dependencies, CI, package metadata, or code download paths. (sha=e8416d4a36fba1da257bb2343d007c9f63600180)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `src/agents/log.ts` for an `agents` subsystem logger and rewires `agent-command` to derive `agents/agent-command` from it.

PR surface: Source +3. Total +3 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is an internal refactor rather than a bug report. The behavior equivalence is source-checkable through the existing child logger contract and the PR's terminal output.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer review and landing with refreshed checks.

target.title: feat(agents): add top-level subsystem logger
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `src/agents/log.ts` exporting a `SubsystemLogger` for the `"agents"` subsystem
- Follows the existing pattern of `src/agents/cli-runner/log.ts` and `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/logger.ts`
- Wires `agent-command.ts` as the first caller via `agentsLog.child("agent-command")` — identical runtime behavior to the previous `createSubsystemLogger("agents/agent-command")` call

## Verification

CI run [26479984925](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26479984925) on head `e8416d4a36`: `build-artifacts` ✅ `check-dependencies` ✅ `check-prod-types` ✅ `checks-node-agentic-agents` ✅

Local terminal proof (tsx v4.22.3, Node v24.15.0):

```
$ node_modules/.bin/tsx --eval "
import { log } from './src/agents/log.ts';
console.log('subsystem:', log.subsystem);
const child = log.child('agent-command');
console.log('child subsystem:', child.subsystem);
console.log('isEnabled(info):', log.isEnabled('info'));
"
subsystem: agents
child subsystem: agents/agent-command
isEnabled(info): true
```

`log.child("agent-command")` produces `"agents/agent-command"` — same subsystem string as the previous direct `createSubsystemLogger` call.
target.author: caulslorenzo1-png
